page: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the c8a.4xlarge demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU tests, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression. Multi-core performance is also strong, with Geekbench multi-core scores in the top 10% and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression in the top 25%. For specialized workloads, the instance achieves top-tier results in image and text processing, as well as small-model LLM inference prompt processing and text generation. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the average tier.

Given its compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2 and the absence of bundled local storage or GPUs, the c8a.4xlarge offers a resource-dense profile optimized for CPU-bound tasks. It provides high single-core efficiency without the overhead of unneeded memory or storage resources. This configuration is highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads such as web serving, single-threaded data compression, image processing, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but provides an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance, single-threaded compression, and small-model LLM inference, alongside strong multi-core capabilities. This hardware profile is highly efficient for CPU-bound tasks, making it well-suited for web serving, image processing, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz, 32 GB of DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for CPU-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 AMD EPYC cores, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for intensive single-core and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 16-core AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Features 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Optimized for web serving, image processing, and small-model LLM inference
